title: 海地朗姆酒巨头与装瓶商警告：帮派肆虐或将引发“新失落地带”
content: Haitian rum producers and bottling companies have warned that escalating gang violence could create a “new lost zone,” jeopardizing production, distribution, and safety. The firms fear that increased conflict will disrupt operations, damage infrastructure, and harm personnel, potentially crippling the industry’s supply chain and market presence. They urge authorities to curb violence to protect the region’s economic stability.

title: 人工智能会夺走你的工作，还是让你成为亿万富翁
content: The article discusses how artificial intelligence can either eliminate traditional jobs or create new opportunities that may lead to significant wealth. It examines the dual impact of AI on employment and economic prospects, highlighting both the threat to routine roles and the potential for high-paying, AI-driven ventures.

title: 一笔巨额交易将如何改变电梯行业
content: A large, undisclosed merger or acquisition promises to reshape the elevator sector by accelerating technology integration, expanding global reach, and increasing capital availability for innovation. The deal is expected to drive efficiencies, standardize safety and maintenance practices, and potentially shift market dynamics toward consolidated, vertically integrated operations, impacting suppliers, operators, and regulatory frameworks.

title: 人工智能与认知投降的危险
content: The article discusses the risk that artificial intelligence may lead to "cognitive surrender," where humans overly rely on AI for decision‑making, potentially eroding critical thinking, responsibility, and autonomy. It warns that such dependence could compromise our ability to evaluate information, make sound judgments, or account for ethical concerns, thereby undermining individual and societal resilience.

title: 特朗普与习近平之间的贸易战威胁如何演变为一场更为低调的竞争
content: The article explains how the sharp trade conflict between former U.S. President Trump and Chinese President Xi initially sparked intense, public threats of a full‑blown trade war. Over time, the confrontations have dimmed, moving from headline‑making accusations to more subtle economic competitions. The U.S. and China now engage in quieter, less visible rivalries—such as technology restrictions and strategic market influence—rather than overt trade embargoes, reflecting a shift toward less confrontational yet still competitive tactics.

title: Anthropic 真是绝了，刚收完钱就把我踢了
content: Anthropic paid for an account, but the user was immediately disconnected and could no longer log in. The user reports that the money was still being deducted despite losing access. A similar incident involved an agricultural company whose accounts were shut down after one user alleged a violation, leading to lost data and continued billing. The author criticizes the lack of notice and backup policies, warning others to safeguard digital assets when using AI services.

content: The United Arab Emirates has announced its decision to leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC). title: Workday’s Last Workday? AI and the Future of Enterprise Software
content: Elena Burger interviews Joe Schmidt about how AI will reshape enterprise software, using Workday as an example. They argue that many current SaaS systems feel broken, that platform shifts can overturn entire categories, and that AI-native agents could radically change workflow, permission, and system management. The discussion highlights limits of legacy SaaS, questions over touted AI‑revenue, and the vulnerability of even defensible software businesses to replatforming.

title: HappyHorse没有惊喜
content: HappyHorse, Alibaba’s new 15‑second video model, launched a gray test on April 27 after topping AI video benchmarks. Despite its 15‑billion‑parameter size, it falls short of competitors like Seedance 2.0, offering lower filmic realism and prompt fidelity. The model supports 720p/1080p output at modest rates, but without significant technical or commercial differentiation. In a crowded market demanding rapid innovation, HappyHorse remains a modest, early‑stage entrant.

title: 雅阁耗尽了“专车红利”
content: The Honda Accord once dominated China’s B‑class market, thriving on fuel efficiency and becoming a staple for ride‑hailing services. By 2019 it sold 223,000 units, topping the segment. However, the surge of electric vehicles, market saturation, and fatigue of the ride‑hailing niche eroded its appeal. Launches of new, underwhelming Accord models and heavy discounting failed to reverse declining sales, while higher maintenance costs and low resale value further weakened the brand’s position.
eventKey: 雅阁耗尽了“专车红利”
normalizedTitle: 雅阁耗尽了“专车红利”

title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
content: In episode 242 of the LWiAI podcast, hosts Andrey Kurenkov and Jeremie Harris review recent AI developments. OpenAI unveiled ChatGPT Images 2.0, capable of accurate text and screenshot reproductions. Alibaba accelerated its Qwen 3.6 Max to API-only use, while Moonshot AI (Kimi K2.6) and Minimax (M 2.7) released strong open‑source models. Google launched Deep Research agents and added Gemini 3.1 Pro; Mozilla used Claude to fix 271 Firefox bugs. Business news included SpaceX–Cursor deal, Cerebras IPO filing, and Anthropic’s $5 B Amazon investment. The episode also covers synthetic media regulation and various policy updates.
source: Last Week in AI - Last Week in AI

title: Anthropic Claude Code 大幅涨价：从预估每日 6 美元翻倍至 13 美元
content: Anthropic has doubled the daily cost of its Claude Code AI programming assistant, raising the estimated expense from about $6 to $13 per active developer per day. The pricing, based on token usage, reflects a broader increase in AI service costs. A recent adjustment to the pricing page drew user complaints, which Anthropic said was a test for a small percentage of new users. The company’s growth head noted current subscription plans no longer match current usage levels.
